ground_truth: Merge freeze for non-critical PRs starts after Thursday due to the mobile release-branch cut.
probe_query: Is there a merge freeze coming up, and why?

retrieved: [playbook] Recipe for merge-timing decisions under a freeze: (1) Convert the relative deadline to an absolute date — in this trajectory 'after Thursday' with today=2026-04-21 (Tuesday) resolves to freeze start 2026-04-24 (Friday), so Thu 2026-04-23 is the last shippable day. (2) Classify the PR: 'nice-to-have refactor' = non-critical, explicitly covered by the freeze. (3) One-line answer format: ship today (or before Thu 2026-04-23) to land before the freeze, otherwise wait until the mobile release branch is cut. Always convert relative dates to absolute dates when saving project memory about the freeze. | User asks whether a non-critical / nice-to-have PR should ship given a stated merge-freeze deadline tied to another team's release cut. | Captures the concrete date conversion and decision heuristic needed to answer merge-timing questions against a freeze window, so a future similar question can be answered directly.
